W.label: the wet floor along the water side · smells like brine

w.label: the boathouse water · deeper than it looks · you can see the pond bottom four feet down

c.label: the racked canoes · six of them · each with a name painted on the bow

j.label: a stack of orange life jackets · some new, most from the seventies

l.label: a hurricane lantern hanging from the beam · lit · someone was here recently

C.label: a padlocked wooden chest at the back · older than the boathouse

s.label: a rough plank shelf with rope, hooks, oil, a broken oar, and a coffee tin labeled 'NAILS'

N.label: a torn front-page clipping under the coffee tin · yellowed · the Tillamook Herald · July 25, 1976

D.label: back out to the pond dock

notes: The boathouse interior · racked canoes at center, life jackets below, an open water bay along the south side that connects to Alder Pond, a plank shelf on the east wall with rope + hooks + oil + a broken oar + a coffee tin, and a padlocked wooden chest at the back that's older than the boathouse itself · Wilson's 1988 logbook is inside it.  A hurricane lantern hangs from a beam.  Someone was here recently.
